observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
Re-issue demand letters to the accountable persons of the dormant accounts, at least three consecutive times.   File these demand letters properly as evidence of Management’s effort to collect the said receivables; and
If no settlements are made after the issuance of demand letters, request these accounts for write-off to the Audit Team with the documentary requirements prescribed by COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Review the records of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in all funds and post the necessary adjustments to present the correct balances of the reciprocal accounts; and
Make a reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts every year-end to present an adjusted and reconciled balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management:
Demand the presentation of the items from the representative of the AFS to ascertain their existence and conditions, and for the proper documentation of issuances/reissuances thereof.   If there are missing items, require the concerned person to personally answer thereof pursuant to the MOA, and to recover the possible loss incurred;
Consider the recall of the custody thereof from the organization and to transfer instead to the Tourism Office to remove any conflict of interest and to ensure that the assets are safeguarded from loss and misuse.  Require the Tourism Office and the GSO to formulate guidelines for the use of the costumes and instruments by the different organization and IP group of the Municipality in order to promote the culture of the locality or possibly for income-generating activity; and
Require the GSO to issue the proper ICS of these items and to renew thereof every after three years, to ensure that the accountability thereof is rightfully documented and established.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer:
Identify all the PPEs assigned to the schools and district offices, and prepare the necessary documents for the transfer of records and accountability thereof, to the DepEd, for their proper accounting and recording.  Cancel the PARs issued to the superintendents, principals, and teachers upon their transfer; and
Henceforth, to avoid accumulation of PPEs under SEF, if PPEs were in the custody and control of district offices and schools, immediately transfer the ownership thereof to the DepEd following the proper procedures, and record the transfer to the proper expense account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Make an appointment with the GSIS Bayombong to identify the discrepancies of their records and the LGU, and to make the necessary adjustments to reconcile both records;
Ascertain that the government and personal shares are properly withheld and remitted to GSIS; and
Coordinate with the GSIS to submit an update for any changes of status, positions, salaries and other pertinent information of every employee to avoid inconsistencies of records between the Municipality and GSIS BO records.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the MDRRMC to review/evaluate the MDRRMP and consider disregarding the originally programmed PPAs that remain unimplemented and realign those which are no longer responsive to the effective and efficient delivery of response and recovery services and reduction of disaster risks of the Municipality, and identify those that contribute to the achievement of the same.  Instruct the MDRRMO to accumulate all the savings and unprogrammed funds of the MDRRMF to finance in carrying out these PPAs;
To have a dialogue with the SB to consider the issuance of SB ordinance in the approval or adoption of SAIPs with regard to the revised PPAs of the MDRRMP, instead of a resolution, in order to timely implement the projects outlined in the MDRRMP, pursuant to Item 6.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1; and
Instruct the MDRRM Officer to review the processes and systems put in place in the implementation of the MDRRMP to improve and hasten their execution to increase the utilization rate of the MDRRMF so that the benefits that can be derived therefrom will redound to the community and constituents of Aritao.

We recommended that the LCE, being the primary responsible official of the Municipality, require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and all other concerned personnel to submit immediately the unsubmitted financial reports, transaction documents (vouchers and official receipts) for post-audit.  Strict observance of the prescribed submission dates of mandatory reports as required under Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 is highly recommended.
We further recommended that the Management thru the Accounting Unit ensure to perform their main official function and responsibilities by producing an organized, complete and accurate financial reports as an end-product of the accounting system, and submit thereof to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and the GSO to post the RFQs of the procurements through SVP with above P50,000.00 ABCs in the PhilGEPS, in the agency’s website, and at any conspicuous place reserved for such purpose in the premises of the Municipality, in adherence with of Item 8 (b) of Annex H of the RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all procurements requiring the conduct of pre-procurement conference must be held to ensure that the procurements are well planned and unnecessary risks are avoided.

We recommended that the Management:
Fill the vacancies which are provided with budget with qualified individuals by following the process of recruitment to reduce the list of casuals and job orders that are doing regular agency functions and to reduce also the charges in the maintenance and other operating expenses which could be used for other social services of the LGU. This will boost their morale and they may enjoy the benefits of a regular employee. Without regular hiring, continuous separation of employees due to retirement, dismissal, resignation and death, and new created positions, vacant positions will accumulate which affects the services of the Municipality;
Design policies or procedures that will maximize the services of both the permanent and non-permanent personnel involved in the administrative, clerical and utility works, such as assignment of multi-tasks to personnel (dual functions or serving two or more offices), centralizing personnel that provides janitorial services or centralizing procurement activities to GSO in line with appropriate rules and regulations;
Stop engaging the services of excessive and unnecessary casual, COS and JOs, otherwise, payment of their wages will be disallowed in audit; and
Instruct the HRMO to:
Evaluate the accomplishments of each administrative, clerical, and utility workers to identify duplication or redundant responsibilities, and to immediately recommend the termination of the contracts to avoid incurrence of additional unnecessary expenditures.  At the same time, asses the needs of administrative, clerical and utility of each office and designate a reasonable number of non-permanent personnel to complement the existing permanent personnel to deliver effective and efficient services; and
Assess the existing organizational structure of the Municipality, taking into consideration the service requirements and financial capability thereof, to reduce the hiring of casual and COS/JO personnel.

We recommended that Management:
Stop the imprudent expenditure for Team Building and any similar activity, and ensure that food, accommodations, and other incidental expenses must not exceed the maximum rate of daily traveling expenses prescribed by EO No. 77; and
Always adopt competitive bidding as mode of procurement of all goods and services, except if attending circumstances warrant adoption of other alternative mode of procurement and complies with the conditions prescribed by the IRR of RA 9184, to generate more savings that can be used for other social services of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Refrain from conducting out-of-town seminars/trainings, conferences, unless extremely necessary, to avoid incurrence of unnecessary expenditures pursuant to the government mandate to adopt austerity measures in government disbursements;
Justify in writing why the expenditures amounting to P728,600.00 should not be disallowed in audit, considering that it constitutes unnecessary expenditure defined by COA Circular No. 2012-003; and
Require all officials and employees of the Municipality to be frugal and cautious in the disbursement of government funds to avoid wastage and to observe austerity measures in all activities of the government.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer continue to monitor all the collectors and liquidating officer with their collections and deposits/remittance to safeguard government funds from loss and misappropriation and to help them carry out their duties and responsibilities. Further, to the Liquidating Officer and collectors, continue the daily deposit and remittance in conformity with Local Treasury guidelines.

We commended the Municipal Treasury Office in adhering with the guidelines when it comes to the handling of cash and following the recommendations of the audit team throughout the past years.
observations.managementAction: Management continued the daily and intact remittance and deposit of collections.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management for following continuously the guidelines set forth by the higher authority like the COA in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance and encouraged the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer to continue the good practices in the grant, utilization and liquidation and monthly reconciliation of cash advances. With this, the management is not only safeguarding the government coffer but also protecting its employees from possible administrative, civil, and/or criminal charges if cash advances were not liquidated properly.
observations.managementAction: The Disbursing Officer continued its compliance with regard to the guidelines of cash advance and its liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the enumerated documentary requirements, along with the duly acknowledged SRA and AHDP payroll to corroborate the transaction entered into and to prevent audit suspension. Further, be reminded that all disbursements must be supported with complete documentation as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and in accordance with the provision of the law stated above.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Health Office submitted the needed documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Inventory Committee to submit on or before the prescribed period the RPCI and RPCPPE and incorporate therein the status of the properties existing during the actual physical count. Continue to use the RIS, RSMI, PAR, WMR and Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) and fill-up properly these documents so that accountability of inventories and properties could be determined easily and loss or wastage will be avoided. It is recommended to conduct the actual count of inventories twice a year due to the susceptibility of wastage especially inventories of drugs and medicines, agricultural products and relief goods which are prone to expiry.
observations.managementAction: The RPCPPE were submitted beyond the prescribed period. RPCI were not submitted since no inventory count was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant (1) analyze the pertinent accounts and make necessary verification against previous financial reports and schedules to determine the details of these payables, (2) confirm these amounts with  the claimants if they have receivable amounts from the Municipality, (3) ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents, and (4) drop from the books those accounts that were not supported with necessary documents or considered invalid claims.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Engineer strictly follow the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects, Volume 3 in preparing the POWs and Cost Estimates which will be the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract (ABC) of infrastructure projects. Please be reminded also that any excess expenditures that were not in compliance with laws, rules and regulations are disallowable in audit.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Development Council (1) plan the PPAs according to the needs of the constituents, (2) coordinate with all the concerned offices for the effective and early completion of implementation of PPAs, (3) determine further the bottleneck of the delay or what causes the slow implementation of PPAs and resolve this by conferring to all the offices / committee / department involved, and (4) ensure the full implementation of PPAs included in the Local Expenditure Program for the year, continuing projects and PPAs in Special Education Fund and Trust Fund at optimum.

We recommended that the Management exert more effort in consolidating all the requisitions of needed items quarterly. Apply the guidelines to all kinds of procurement except for the project based and emergency ones.

We recommended that the Management withhold five (5) percent VAT from the claims of NUVELCO. Observe the proper withholding of taxes as prescribed by the BIR so that the government will not lose revenue.

We recommended that the Management fill the vacant positions regularly in order to reduce the hiring of daily wage earners. The JOs/COS may fill the vacant positions as long as the process of hiring are followed. This will boost their morale and they may enjoy the benefits of a regular employee. Further, with the Mandanas Ruling, more funds will be downloaded to the Municipality and that would result to more regular job opportunities. Without regular hiring, continuous separation of employees due to retirement, dismissal, resignation and death, and new created positions, vacant positions will accumulate.

We recommended that the Management consolidate payment telephone expenses in a month and schedule the emission testing, registration and payment of insurance coverage of all the municipal vehicles in order to have a single or scheduled payment to minimize the use of office supplies such as checks and bond papers and maximize time management. Further, all procurements of common supplies, cleaning materials, medical supplies, office equipment and appliances, information and communication technology equipment must be consolidated per month or per quarter as provided in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). Moreover, ensure that all documentary requirements are submitted and signed before the processing of payment.  Finally, be prudent in expending government funds for the benefit of the people of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management (1) fast track the submission of Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), (2) update all the PAR of properties and ensure that all PARs attached in the disbursement vouchers were duly signed, (3) make a list for all the returned and unserviceable properties, (4) all vehicles not entrusted to department heads and municipal officials should be in the custody of the Supply Officer or General Services Office, and (5) custody and approval of supplies should not be assigned in one department but create the Office of the General Services Officer for the purpose, otherwise the Municipal Treasurer shall do the task as ruled in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAs) for Local Governments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) strictly comply with procedural guidelines and must ensure to seek authority from the SB to demolish buildings and structures owned by the Municipality. Likewise, be remined that all disposal of supplies and properties should conform with Rule 22 or the Reporting of Disposable Supplies and Property under COA Circular No. 92-386, Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management of LGUs.

We recommended that the Management ensure completeness of the required supporting documents on every claim of terminal leave benefit before the processing the payment. Submit all the approved leave application form and service records of all the retiree mentioned in the table above. Failure to submit such will prompt the audit team to issue Notice of Suspension and for all the succeeding transactions without complete documentation. Further, please be reminded of COA Circular No. 2012-001 as guidance of documentary requirements for common government transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer continue to monitor all the collectors and liquidating officer with their collections and deposits/remittance to safeguard government funds from loss and misappropriation and to help them carry out their duties and responsibilities. Further, to the Liquidating Officer and collectors, continue the daily deposit and remittance in conformity with Local Treasury guidelines.

We recommended that the Management submit the enumerated documentary requirements, along with the duly acknowledged SRA and AHDP payroll to corroborate the transaction entered into and to prevent audit suspension. Further, be reminded that all disbursements must be supported with complete documentation as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and in accordance with the provision of the law stated above.

We recommended Management to:
(1) adjust the recorded expenses to appropriate Assets accounts; 
(2) issue PAR for all the procured goods totaling ₱506,900.00; and 
(3) be reminded of the IPSAS 17 so that all goods procured qualified as PPE will be recorded under PPE and the semi-expandable items under COA Circular No. 2005-002 will not be recorded under PPE but under Inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office Adjusted already the accounts affected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accounting Office to adjust the amount recorded in the account “Due from NGOs/POs from ₱318,800.00 to ₱637,600.00 and credit the affected Expenses account. Necessary adjustment should also be made after the completion of the project or after the set period under the MOA. We further recommended to the Accreditation Committee and Cooperative Affairs Office to direct the concerned NGOs/POs of the submission of utilization report for proper validation and verification, so that the Accounting Office will be guided on the adjustment to be made as well as the concerned NGOs/POs on their accountability to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Affected accounts were adjusted.
Supplies were recorded as assets first and recorded as expense upon release from the supply officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accounting Office to rectify the accounts used in recording the transaction through a correcting entry. Necessary adjustment should be made to record the effect of liquidation documents submitted thereafter. We further recommended the Management to advise the concerned Implementing Agency of the submission of utilization report / liquidation documents for proper validation and verification of the Audit Team, for appropriate adjustment by the Accounting Office and for the concerned Implementing Agency to prove that they complied with the terms of their accountability to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The official receipt from the League of Municipalities served as the liquidation document.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accounting Office to make the adjustment to accounts Other MOOE and Trust Liabilities both in the amount of ₱110,000.00. Necessary adjustment should be made after the completion of the project or after the set period (one year). We further recommended to the concerned office to monitor regularly the project implementation of PWD members to ensure that the government funds given them were really used for the intended purpose and to remind their accountability to the Municipality and advise them to settle their obligation as soon as the set period of time has lapsed as stipulated in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Affected accounts were adjusted and the assistance were being monitored.
Concerned accountable employee submitted the needed documents for liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to account all the remaining items of donations in kind received and provide all the necessary documents to the Accounting Office for its recording. Further, direct all offices to inform the concerned offices if they receive anything in favor of the Municipality for its proper costing and recording to avoid repetition of the circumstances in the past and possible conflict with the law. The Accounting Office is advised to record all the donations received by the Municipality be it in cash or in kind. Acquisition cost or appraised value of donations in kind should be determined for its recording.
observations.managementAction: Remaining items were accounted and recorded

observations.recommendation: We recommended that every office in-charge of meetings and seminars for the programs/activities should plan and coordinate thoroughly the events with all the participants. Include a notice in the advisory, office orders, or any letter of invitation to the participants that they are responsible to confirm their attendance to the said meetings or seminars so that the budget for representation expense for the activities processed beforehand will be adjusted and the changes are communicated to the winning bidder/supplier.
observations.managementAction: Seminars and activities were being coordinated efficiently to all the participants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to stop payments of penalties or surcharges since these are not necessary expenditures in the course of operation of the municipality and ensure that all disbursements do not contain penalties and surcharges or any fee of the same nature to avoid adverse remark that may smear the image of the good governance of the municipality. Further, renew all licenses in time to prevent the accumulation of penalties and surcharges. Succeeding disbursements that contain penalties and surcharges shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the payment of penalties. Incurred penalties were being shouldered by the person responsible in the incurrence of the penalty.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the requisitioning office to indicate the description/technical specifications of items to be procured in the Purchase Request, Abstract of Quotations and other documentary requirements in order to apprise the approving authorities and the suppliers of the specifications of items needed by the end user. Moreover, we recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to monitor all the purchase requests and notify the requisitioning office immediately of the lacking description/specification before processing the request.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to indicate technical specifications, however, there were procurements that lack description or technical specifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to consolidate the needed purchases quarterly as shown in the APP and PPMP. The Technical Working Group should recommend to the BAC the mode of procurement on Purchase Requests in compliance with Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-386 which could give more advantage to the government through the generation of savings that could augment the Municipal coffers for other programs and activities. Further, Management is encouraged to formulate a simple control system so that all the needs of the offices are being consolidated and procured immediately without delay as well as adhere with the provisions of the law stated above.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to consolidate all the procurements, but unsuccessful on some items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the Accreditation Committee, Cooperative Affairs Office and the NGO/PO to comply with the documentary requirements stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in relation to the requirement of accreditation due to the  grant of financial assistance to the NGOs/POs. We also recommended that Management ensures that the organizations have a stable financial condition and that the funds provided by the Management shall not be its sole source of funds used in operations as evidenced by its audited financial statements. The management should also ensure that the organizations granted with public funds have the capability to implement livelihood projects and programs. Include in the MOA the condition that the whole transferred amount is subject for liquidation and submission of utilization report at the completion of the project or after three (3) years as prescribed in COA Circular 2007-001.
observations.managementAction: Documentary requirements were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to plan thoroughly each and every procurement, as it should be within the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and minimize the reimbursement of officials and employees. Consider only the possible transactions that can be truly reimbursed.  Plan ahead of time the procurements so that purchases of supplies won’t be only made when the stocks are at critical level so that Management would not resort in reimbursing expenses for emergency purchases.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to follow the recommendations. And, in case disbursements were made through reimbursements, the corresponding taxes were being withheld.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management posts all the accumulated reports prepared and submitted to DBM and all appropriation ordinance and financial documents related to Bayanihan Fund. Further, comply with the list of documents that are required to be posted quarterly and annually in adherence with the Full Disclosure Policy of the Department of the Interior and Local Government.
observations.managementAction: Reports regarding Bayanihan Fund were posted already in three (3) conspicuous public places.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management especially the offices which approve the loans of employees to abide with the rules and regulations regarding the minimum monthly net take home pay of ₱5,000.00. Further, Management should require its employees to secure certification of net-take home pay for the two (2) immediately preceding months of loan application from the Accountant as a prerequisite to processing of loans by eliminating the negative strategy made by employees just to obtain a certification from the Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped approving loans when the computed net take home pay of the employees will be below the threshold. Employees that were receiving below the net take home pay were those have approved loans before this observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to adopt a system of payroll payment through the ATM of Government Banks for all the salaries, bonuses and emoluments due to officials and employees. The ATM payroll system provides convenience on the part of management since payroll payments are distributed from the payroll account maintained in an authorized government depository bank to the individual account of the regular employees. Convenience also on the part of the officials and employees since the transfer of communicable diseases will be contained. Lastly, convenience on the part of the Disbursing Officer because the bulk of her cash advances will be lessened which means lesser accountability.
observations.managementAction: Salaries and some bonuses were given through the ATM. Honoraria, RATA, allowances, etc. were still given through cash.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from maintaining unliquidated cash advances at the end of the year or liquidate as soon as the purpose of which had been served as required in Section 89 of PD No. 1445. Monitor all cash advances and advice all the accountable officers of the responsibility with respect to the liquidation of their cash advances. Further, be reminded that unused cash advance shall be refunded at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Management still have unliquidated cash advances at the end of the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 6 of Part II

observations.managementAction: Management already recorded their collections and deposits during the year of incurrence.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to (1) transfer the amount of ₱802,377.42 from the TF to GF so that the amount will be considered in the preparation of supplemental appropriation ordinance, (2) update the investment plan for five (5) years of the special trust fund for disaster, and (3) consider the needs of the 22 barangays of the Municipality and embed these needs in the investment plan for disaster to fully utilize the remaining special trust fund in CY 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management transferred already the unspent STF aging more than 5 years to the General Fund. Investment Plans were updated as to the needs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management collect immediately the amount due from the employees and contractor so that the money will revert back to the government’s coffer for proper management and refrain from using the government resources for personal purposes. Demand the payment of the paid income tax from the concerned personnel, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued for the overpayment of salaries.
observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱20,873.17 was already collected, however, there were receivables for the year that were not collected at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (1) reconcile with the different Government Agencies concerned if the LGU has outstanding receivable account in their books in order to identify properly the treatment of the fund, (2) if the PPAs were already implemented and there were still remaining fund balances for return or request approval from the source agencies to use the remaining funds for other PPAs, and (3) identify the bottleneck of the slow implementation of PPAs and see to it that the bottleneck were addressed in order to fast track the implementation of PPAs for the benefit of the people of Aritao.
observations.managementAction: Management had difficult time to reconcile and implement some programs and projects due to the pandemic, but Management tried to implement the projects funded by the National Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to (1) collect immediately the amount of ₱150,250.00 from the concerned three employees, (2) see to it that the payments of all farmers from the PNPL program and the Hyta Seeds roll over scheme are all being receipted by the Treasury Office and properly recorded and accounted for in the municipal books, and (3) verify and validate the amount ₱329,185.00 from the listed borrowers so that the responsibility to collect the said amount will be assumed by the Municipal Agriculturist who is responsible in the fund disbursement in addition to the amount of ₱1,154,185.00 previously officially assumed by her.
observations.managementAction: Management collected these receivables through salary deductions. Now, there are only two employees who have outstanding liability in favor of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to inquire on employees with more than 24 years in service if they could identify the liable persons and put with remarks “separated from service with/without clearance, resigned and deceased” on their listed names. If these liable persons were found, collection must be made, however, if they could not be found or found but insolvent, Management is encouraged to find any possible and legal way out to reduce, if not eliminate, these receivables in disallowances since these are already dormant accounts  of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management inquired possible ways from the Commission Proper to remove these Receivable – Disallowance due to non-availability of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) formulate strategic collection schemes so that the amount of ₱3,252,261.99 lodged in the receivable accounts will decrease until such time that the amount recorded in the Receivable accounts were only the uncollected RPT and SET for the current year, (2) coordinate with the Punong Barangays with regard to the collection of RPT and SET from delinquent tax payers for easier access, (3) call the attention of all the departments involved to address this deficiency, and (4) see to it that the Receivable accounts will already have gradual decrease for the next years  and avoid the trend in CYs 2015-2019 that the Receivable accounts remained increasing every year.
observations.managementAction: Management cannot fully implement the collection schemes due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to see to it that the total amount of ₱625,030.75 be collected from the identified contractors as shown in Annex N and remit the collected amount to the BIR or require the contractor to submit to the accounting office a certification from the BIR that the contractors has no tax deficiency or cleared from payment of taxes. Observe the proper withholding of taxes as prescribed by the BIR so that the government will not lose revenue.
observations.managementAction: Management already withholds the correct percentage of VAT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (1) analyze if the warranty securities posted were valid to cover the defects or damages that maybe incurred during the liability period, and (2) require all the contractors to post valid warranty security as prescribed by the procurement law.
observations.managementAction: Management has been requiring the contractors to post warranty bond after the release of retention money.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to (1) be consistent in the application of the deduction of retention money to every contractors, (2) deduct, in every progress billing the 10% retention money until 50% of the value works, provided that works were satisfactorily done and on schedule, and (3) hold the retention money until contractors post warranty security for the completed projects.
observations.managementAction: Correct retention money is  already being deducted in every progress billing for every infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) inform the audit team on any progress on the repair committed to be performed by the contractor in the Concreting of Core Local Access Road (Comon-Calitlitan), (2) see to it that all projects implemented by the municipality were being supervised by the project engineers and all deficiencies that will be noticed or all deviations from the plans/specifications must be communicated to the contractors and check if these deficiencies or deviations are being rectified, and (3) may create an inspectorate team who are experts with the technicalities of projects to be inspected so as the management is assured that the government funds are being disbursed without irregularity.
observations.managementAction: Management had mandated the contractor to rectify the defects of the road constructions and there were already repairs done by the contractor, however, it still not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management follow proper procedures in government transactions, accomplish properly all the government forms, support all government expenditures with proper documentation, provide and maintain rules in the use of motor vehicles and in giving financial assistance to charitable constituents, and finally, be prudent in expending government funds for the benefit of the people of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to follow the proper procedures in government transactions, however, there are still some procedures that ar not followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (1) continue to address the identified constraints in the implementation of PPAs and provide corrective measures to guarantee the high implementation of PPAs, if not full, (2) fully implement the computation of liquidated damages on the claim of the contractors so that they will be forced to finish their contracts within the expected time and the municipality may generate other source of income, and (3) monitor regularly the progress reports of all the officers and employees concerned in the implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management had difficult time to implement the PPAs of the Municipality due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to (1) identify if there is shortage of engineers to prepare the POWs and address this problem so that the bottleneck of non-implementation will be lessened, if not eliminated, and (2) heighten the monitoring of implementation of PPAs so as to remind everyone of their responsibility and obligation that contribute to the accomplishment of tasks.
observations.managementAction: Management hired additional engineers to fast-track the preparation of POWs. However, the implementation was restricted due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management - (1) for CY 2020 onwards, stop the remittance of Pag-IBIG contribution in excess of the maximum contribution of an employer; (2) shall refund to the municipality the excess employer’s contribution erroneously remitted in January to May, 2020; and, (3) religiously follow the guidelines of Pag-IBIG Fund for the benefit not only of the employees but also its employer, the Municipality of Aritao.
observations.managementAction: Management had already collected the excessive contribution through salary deduction, except for some employees. However, they have already signified their intention to refund the excess contribution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in granting financial assistance, Management should prioritize individuals / groups who are in dire situation to uplift not only their living status but also their morale; and (2) stop this irregular or excessive transactions to avoid any disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped already the granting of financial assistance to JOs for their Christmas and Year-end party.

observations.recommendation: We would like to commend the municipality for implementing the activities / programs under RA No. 9003. Likewise, we recommended Management to (1) continue to implement proper waste management through the segregation and collection of solid waste, (2) strengthen the function of MRF and VCF to recycle and reduce waste, and (3) monitor regularly the implementation of RA No. 9003 on the barangay level so that the efforts and resources of the Municipality will contribute to better community living.
observations.managementAction: Management continued the implementation of RA No. 9003 in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) make sure that all the transactions incurred for the year are recorded in same year, (2) deposit at the end of the year all the collections receipted for that year, if possible, and (3) present the actual balance of the accounts in order to present fairly the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management still recorded the deposits in January 2020 as deposits in December 2019. Thus, the Cash in Bank in 2019 were overstated and the Cash in Local Treasury was under stated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) issue demand letters to the accountable persons to collect the amount of P49,168.98, (2) assess the collectability of these amounts and provide allowance for impairment of these receivables if there are evidence that the receivables are impaired to present the true status of the financial position, and (3) monitor the cash advances and liquidations of accountable officers and other collectibles of the LGU in order to avoid the use of government funds for private purpose.
observations.managementAction: Management did not issue demand letters to the accountable persons due to humanitarian purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality provide legal basis of granting the HEDRA otherwise, same shall be returned back to the government coffers for having been disbursed in violation of government laws, rules and regulations.  The Municipality is also advised to refrain from duplicating the granting of benefit of same nature in future operations without legal basis.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted their basis to the Audit Team. However, the HEDRA was disallowed and the liable persons appealed to the Regional Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) submit to the audit team all perfected contracts with documentary requirements for CY 2018 within 10 calendar days from receipt hereof, and (2) for CY 2019, comply with the prescribed period of submission to avoid any administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 4.0 of the same circular.
observations.managementAction: Only POs were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management present in “Cash in Local Treasury” account all collections that are still in the custody of the accountable officers at the end of the year and be reminded that all transactions incurred for the year must be recorded in the same year in order to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended Management to (1) transfer the amount of ₱802,377.42 from the TF to GF so that the amount will be considered in the preparation of supplemental appropriation ordinance, (2) update the investment plan for five (5) years of the special trust fund for disaster, and (3) consider the needs of the 22 barangays of the Municipality and embed these needs in the investment plan for disaster to fully utilize the remaining special trust fund in CY 2014.

We recommended that management refrain from maintaining unliquidated cash advances at the end of the year or liquidate as soon as the purpose of which had been served as required in Section 89 of PD No. 1445. Monitor all cash advances and advice all the accountable officers of the responsibility with respect to the liquidation of their cash advances. Further, be reminded that unused cash advance shall be refunded at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management collect immediately the amount due from the employees and contractor so that the money will revert back to the government’s coffer for proper management and refrain from using the government resources for personal purposes. Demand the payment of the paid income tax from the concerned personnel, otherwise a Notice of Disallowance will be issued for the overpayment of salaries.

We recommended that Management (1) reconcile with the different Government Agencies concerned if the LGU has outstanding receivable account in their books in order to identify properly the treatment of the fund, (2) if the PPAs were already implemented and there were still remaining fund balances for return or request approval from the source agencies to use the remaining funds for other PPAs, and (3) identify the bottleneck of the slow implementation of PPAs and see to it that the bottleneck were addressed in order to fast track the implementation of PPAs for the benefit of the people of Aritao.

We recommended Management to (1) collect immediately the amount of ₱150,250.00 from the concerned three employees, (2) see to it that the payments of all farmers from the PNPL program and the Hyta Seeds roll over scheme are all being receipted by the Treasury Office and properly recorded and accounted for in the municipal books, and (3) verify and validate the amount ₱329,185.00 from the listed borrowers so that the responsibility to collect the said amount will be assumed by the Municipal Agriculturist who is responsible in the fund disbursement in addition to the amount of ₱1,154,185.00 previously officially assumed by her.

We recommended Management to inquire on employees with more than 24 years in service if they could identify the liable persons and put with remarks “separated from service with/without clearance, resigned and deceased” on their listed names. If these liable persons were found, collection must be made, however, if they could not be found or found but insolvent, Management is encouraged to find any possible and legal way out to reduce, if not eliminate, these receivables in disallowances since these are already dormant accounts  of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management (1) formulate strategic collection schemes so that the amount of ₱3,252,261.99 lodged in the receivable accounts will decrease until such time that the amount recorded in the Receivable accounts were only the uncollected RPT and SET for the current year, (2) coordinate with the Punong Barangays with regard to the collection of RPT and SET from delinquent tax payers for easier access, (3) call the attention of all the departments involved to address this deficiency, and (4) see to it that the Receivable accounts will already have gradual decrease for the next years  and avoid the trend in CYs 2015-2019 that the Receivable accounts remained increasing every year.

We recommended Management to see to it that the total amount of ₱625,030.75 be collected from the identified contractors as shown in Annex N and remit the collected amount to the BIR or require the contractor to submit to the accounting office a certification from the BIR that the contractors has no tax deficiency or cleared from payment of taxes. Observe the proper withholding of taxes as prescribed by the BIR so that the government will not lose revenue.

We recommended that Management (1) analyze if the warranty securities posted were valid to cover the defects or damages that maybe incurred during the liability period, and (2) require all the contractors to post valid warranty security as prescribed by the procurement law.

We recommended Management to (1) be consistent in the application of the deduction of retention money to every contractors, (2) deduct, in every progress billing the 10% retention money until 50% of the value works, provided that works were satisfactorily done and on schedule, and (3) hold the retention money until contractors post warranty security for the completed projects.

We recommended that the Management (1) inform the audit team on any progress on the repair committed to be performed by the contractor in the Concreting of Core Local Access Road (Comon-Calitlitan), (2) see to it that all projects implemented by the municipality were being supervised by the project engineers and all deficiencies that will be noticed or all deviations from the plans/specifications must be communicated to the contractors and check if these deficiencies or deviations are being rectified, and (3) may create an inspectorate team who are experts with the technicalities of projects to be inspected so as the management is assured that the government funds are being disbursed without irregularity.

We recommended that Management follow proper procedures in government transactions, accomplish properly all the government forms, support all government expenditures with proper documentation, provide and maintain rules in the use of motor vehicles and in giving financial assistance to charitable constituents, and finally, be prudent in expending government funds for the benefit of the people of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (1) continue to address the identified constraints in the implementation of PPAs and provide corrective measures to guarantee the high implementation of PPAs, if not full, (2) fully implement the computation of liquidated damages on the claim of the contractors so that they will be forced to finish their contracts within the expected time and the municipality may generate other source of income, and (3) monitor regularly the progress reports of all the officers and employees concerned in the implementation of PPAs.

We recommended Management to (1) identify if there is shortage of engineers to prepare the POWs and address this problem so that the bottleneck of non-implementation will be lessened, if not eliminated, and (2) heighten the monitoring of implementation of PPAs so as to remind everyone of their responsibility and obligation that contribute to the accomplishment of tasks.

We recommended that Management - (1) for CY 2020 onwards, stop the remittance of Pag-IBIG contribution in excess of the maximum contribution of an employer; (2) shall refund to the municipality the excess employer’s contribution erroneously remitted in January to May, 2020; and, (3) religiously follow the guidelines of Pag-IBIG Fund for the benefit not only of the employees but also its employer, the Municipality of Aritao.

We recommended that in granting financial assistance, Management should prioritize individuals / groups who are in dire situation to uplift not only their living status but also their morale; and (2) stop this irregular or excessive transactions to avoid any disallowance.

We would like to commend the municipality for implementing the activities / programs under RA No. 9003. Likewise, we recommended Management to (1) continue to implement proper waste management through the segregation and collection of solid waste, (2) strengthen the function of MRF and VCF to recycle and reduce waste, and (3) monitor regularly the implementation of RA No. 9003 on the barangay level so that the efforts and resources of the Municipality will contribute to better community living.

We recommended that the Management (1) submit to the audit team the approved extensions of the subjected projects,  if available, to avoid the issuance of notice of charge, (2) for CY 2019 transactions, submit the approved extensions together with the disbursement vouchers to support payment, if there is, and (3) monitor the implementation of projects and advice the contractors to monitor also their compliance with their contracts to avoid the delayed implementation of projects and adverse findings like liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages are being computed and charged against the contractor whenever there is delay in the completion of a project. The liquidated damages were booked as miscellaneous income.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management see to it that every POW prepared by the MEO will be inspected before, during and after the implementation of projects, be it the projects of the Municipality or of the Barangays, stop the preparation of POW if the projects were already implemented and stop the collections of sum of money in consideration of the prepared POWs since it is the duty of the Engineer and its office to supervise the infrastructure development and public works in general of the local government unit. Further, issue a certificate of inspection once projects were finished or for every payment of progress billing.
observations.managementAction: Inspection and supervision were done in some projects which is the reasons that other projects have still defects upon full payment of the contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management see to it that the 20 percent DF were utilized at its best in order to contribute to the socio-economic development of the community of Aritao, prioritize the projects that need public bidding. For the projects that do not require public bidding, management may opt to use the alternative mode of procurement as long as there is a competitive bidding and the process of procurement were not violated. Further, monitor at all times the compliance of the supplier/contractor on the time of completion specified in the contract to avoid the issuance of notice of charge due to liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to increase the rate of implementation, but failed to do so.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) identify the difficulty of the readiness of program of works, (2) review once in a while the plans and programs so as to be informed or reminded on the projects/programs/activities that needs implementation, (3) see to it that the DRRM fund are utilized to maximize its use which will eventually provide benefits in favor of the constituents, and (4) be reminded that DRRM fund will increase because of the unutilized fund in CY 2018 and the current appropriation for CY 2019.
observations.managementAction: They have already identified and reviewed the plans, however, the fund was still under utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management monitor the implementation of GAD PPPs and see to it that the costs of implementation were at least 5 percent of the annual budget to evade sanctions under administrative law, civil service or other appropriate laws as stated in Section 41 of RA 9710. Further, determine the causes of the slow implementation of these PPPs and make corrective measures to deal with these causes, if any.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of PPAs was being monitored however, the implementation of at least 5 percent were quite challenging for the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management and the LSB (1) identify the projects / programs/activities which were not yet implemented and reprogram these if needed, (2) review the total cash available which were not yet programmed due to overshooting of estimated income, and (3) see to it that all projects/programs/activities in the approved budget of SEF were the priority of the schools so that high rate of implementation, if not full, will be met.
observations.managementAction: Local School Board identified priority projects and passed a supplemental budget for the savings. Further, all expenditures were already in line with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management fill vacant positions following the right process to reduce the list of job order / contract of service that are doing regular agency functions and to reduce also the charges in the maintenance and other operating expenses which could be used for other operating expenses or capital expenditures of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Contract of Services were being hired for a limited period of time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) designate a special disbursing officer from the Treasury Office for the social pension of senior citizen and supplemental feeding of day care children. Approval and custody of funds, since incompatible functions, should not be controlled by one office to avoid misappropriation of funds. Further, (2) see to it that the special disbursing officer is properly bonded to secure government funds.
observations.managementAction: Supplemental Feeding fund are being disbursed already in the treasury office and the release of social pension fund was already under the regional office of DSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) ensure the full implementation of projects from NGAs without delay since these are also priority projects of the LGU, (2) identify the bottleneck of the slow movement of funds or the low rate of  implementation and adapt corrective measures to address the problem, and (3) return the unspent amount of the project after the projects are fully implemented to the source agency or request from the latter that the unspent amount will be utilized for other related charges.
observations.managementAction: The projects were already implemented and the excess fund was returned to the source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management update the alpha list from time to time which could be of help in the determination of the correct amount of tax to be remitted, see to it that the amount withheld are posted in the right accounts and be reminded that withheld taxes for the month should be fully remitted within 10 days of the ensuing month to avoid any penalty due to delayed remittances and to evade any administrative sanction.
observations.managementAction: All withheld taxes for the month were remitted on the 10th day of the ensuing month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) diligently monitor the monthly remittance of mandatory deductions and see to it that all these deductions are remitted in full, (2) avoid over remittance at the expense of the LGU of Aritao, and (3) provide any agreement between the LGU of Aritao and the Pag-IBIG fund with regard to the unremitted deducted premiums in the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Monitored the monthly remittance and avoided the over or under remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management (1) continue to reach, if not surpass, the annual estimated collections and (2) re-examine and identify the constraint/s of not realizing the estimated collections in other revenue item especially the building permit, sales in tilapia and livestock, sales in organic fertilizer and equipment rental.
observations.managementAction: Management has realized over 3 million that the target collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management guarantee the high rate of implementation of PPAs as programed in the approved budget, see to it that the funds budgeted for a specific purpose be prioritized for its implementation and follow the guidelines on the utilization of the different funds maintained. We further recommended that the Management return the unexpended balance of trust fund if the project was already implemented or cannot be implemented because of some issues, or request from the transferor of funds that the unexpended balance be used for the implementation of other PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management were not regularly monitoring the PPAs to be mplemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of the Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management asses its compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness and make necessary action to at least comply with the minimum standards to attain the objective of the LGU and the NDRRMC, that is to reduce disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response, and to become qualified to the incentives/prizes sponsored by the different agencies of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management were still trying to fully implement the recommendations of the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management follow the procedure in the procurement process, attach all the documents required with complete data, provide in the price quotation the name of the canvasser and his signature, and avoid the leakage of the quotations of suppliers to other suppliers to promote competitive bidding and transparency.
observations.managementAction: Claims were not yet supported with complete documentations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management inform the contractors/suppliers with the documentary requirements in support of their claims and see to it that these documentary requirements are completed and validated and not made-up before the approval of the claims. Moreover, ensure that the NTP bears the date of receipt of the contractor/supplier as required in the documents in order to determine the target date of completion and to ensure that these contractors/suppliers will not delay the delivery or implementation of the project in order not to delay also the enjoyment of the benefits of these projects by the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management were not implementing the recommendations and claims were not supported with complete documentations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management fast track the implementation of the projects/programs/activities funded for Gender and Development to promote women empowerment and gender equality.

We recommended that the Management process the transfer of ownership if the lots were not yet in the name of the LGU of Aritao since there were already improvements incorporated in these lots. observations.managementAction: Management assured the adherence to the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management verify the liability to the NUVELCO amounting to P178,711.56, advise the latter to file a money claim to the COA Central Office in order for them to be paid if the amount is found to be correct because the LGU should only disburse for the current transactions of the LGU, except for the recorded liabilities. We further recommended that the Accounting Office make a control or mechanism in order to be apprised of the liabilities of the LGU to all of their suppliers.
observations.managementAction: Management expressed their willingness of full compliance which transpired to their action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management deposit immediately the remaining amount of P193,623.00 in the trust fund account with the corresponding recognition in the books of accounts that this amount is for the construction of Monument Wall and for reproduction of copyrighted CD’s and playbills. This action will definitely promote transparency to the collected funds for the fiesta and its corresponding disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the adherence to the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management collate all the unserviceable properties and conduct disposal thereof in compliance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 so as to lessen the bulk of unserviceable properties stocked in the stock room and in the motor pool to accommodate the newly purchased properties or the returned properties that need repair and to generate additional income from sales that could be used to fund other projects/programs/activities.

Therefore, the  Municipal Treasurer, through the Local Chief Executive, should file an application for the disposal to the auditor for inspection and determination if the property is with or without money value, with the aid of Technical Service Office of COA, since, Section 163 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments provides that “The provincial or city general services officer or municipal or barangay treasurer, as the case maybe, shall be responsible for disposal of supplies or property of the local government”.
observations.managementAction: Management had already complied with the recommendation in CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management see to it that every time there are receipts from PhilHealth representing the reimbursement for MCP program, a part of the amount should be allocated for the payment of premiums for additional PhilHealth Yellow Card Holder Category A and this fund allocation must also be implemented and not to accumulate only in the trust fund.
observations.managementAction: Management were trying to set aside funds for the premiums of additional Philhealth Medicare

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management allocate and expend the funds from the PhilHealth in accordance with the PhilHealth Circular No. 20, s. 2012 and see to it that the medicines and drugs to be acquired is in harmony with the Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF). The MHO should also make an estimate on the quantity and kind of medicines that should be bought which should cater to the needs of their patients.

We further recommended that the amount of P794,573.67 be replenished for medicines and drugs from succeeding proceeds from the PhilHealth, particularly the 40% allocation for reagents, medical supplies and equipment.
observations.managementAction: Management have allocated 40% of the receipts for medicines but not yet returned the amount of P794,573.67

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management especially the MHO to secure first the OR as a proof of payment before conducting the physical examination to the client (employee or not an employee of the LGU) and maintain a logbook for the issuance of medical certificate. It is further recommended that a notification to the public should be posted in the MHO (waiting and reception area) citing that an OR must be claimed whenever a person pays in favor of the government.
observations.managementAction: The Health Office was already issuing slip in order for the Treasury Office to issue Official Receipts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refund the excess payment of P99,771.79 and follow the rate and guidelines provided by the Joint Circulars in giving Hazard Pay and Subsistence Allowance.
observations.managementAction: Management had imposed the recommendations, however, at the end of CY 2018, there were two (2) employees that were not fully returned the amount due to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management follow the rate and rules prescribed by the DBM in granting RATA to officials and employees, and be consistent with the submitted documents and reports in support of the claim. We further recommended that the amount of P63,750.00 be refunded since this amount was an excess of what should only be paid to the officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management had already adhered with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management especially the Agriculture Office collect the amount of P2,640,461.00 from the recipients of the seeds in order to continue the program of the LGU and the DA, record all the transactions from the borrowing of the farmers until  the payments, monitor the planting and harvesting season of the farmers to ensure the collection of the borrowings, and lastly follow the provisions stated in the MOA. If the MOA stated that the program will be a plant-now-pay-later (PNPL) scheme, then, this should be implemented impartially and not to selected farmers only. Further, submit the master list of borrowers to the accounting office as base of recording all the debtors of palay seeds to reduce the liability of the Municipal Agriculturist since the whole amount was accounted to her.
observations.managementAction: Management, especially the concerned office have already settled some of the collectibles, however, big amount were still unsettled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management especially the Municipal Agriculture Office to deposit the amount of P84,376.51 reflected in the Statement of Financial Position of the MFT (Balance Sheet), collect the accounts receivable of P372,763.16 from the debtors of the MFT and afterwards deposit the amount collected.

Furthermore, the Accounting Office should establish a receivable from the person concerned/in-charge of the operation amounting to the cash (P84,376.51) and the collectibles (P372,763.16) of the MFT until these are collected and deposited in the account of MFT . After the restitution of the fund, resume the operation of the MFT and designate person/s who is faithful and capable enough to manage the food terminal to avoid loss or misappropriation of the fund and in the end the farmers and consumers are benefited, accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Management set up already a receivable from the concerned person in the amount of P84,376.51, however, the amount of P372,763.16 were not  yet settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management return the amount of P31,188.75 to the MDRRM fund and charge it to the General Fund proper and be guided by the rules and regulations in the utilization of the said fund as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Office supplies used in the office of the MDRRMO were already charged in the MOOE of general fund in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management adhere with the prescribed rate of 1% of IRA, see to it that the amount allocated are disbursed for strengthening and implementing on the programs of LCPC and programs for the children in conflict with the law are also prioritized.
observations.managementAction: Management had already allocated 1% of the IRA for LCPC

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management indicate the quantity and kind of viands, snacks and drinks in the purchase request and in the other documentary requirements in the procurement of meals and snacks since it is a common knowledge that cost of fish, chicken, pork, and beef are not the same, so as the cost of soft drinks, bottled water, fruit juice and other sweetened beverages to justify every disbursement of the government. See to it that all the supporting documents bear all the signature of the office/employee concerned, attach complete attendance with signature, activity design of programs and the minutes of the proceedings in support of the disbursements.

Moreover, the management should be guided by the Declaration of Policy stated in Section 2 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 that “It is the declared policy of the State that all resources of the government shall be managed, expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations, and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition, with a view to ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness, in the operations of the government…”.
observations.managementAction: Management have already complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management adhere to the above-cited regulations and for the Sangguniang Bayan members, instead of submitting a certificate or evidence of service rendered or approved DTR, the attendance in regular sessions and minutes of meetings should be submitted. stop incurrence of the same expenditure in 2017 onward, for this will be disallowed.

Also, implement only those programs, projects and activities that are gender-responsive or those that address gender-related issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to (1) maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and optimally utilize the said fund to help achieve and deliver desirable socio-economic and environmental development and sustainability, (2) ensure that only authorized expenses are charged against the 20% DF, and (3) revisit programs that remained unimplemented under 20% DF and consider revising the PPAs to conform with regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LGU ensured adherence to the provision of the Joint Memorandum Circular to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management regularly prepare the monthly LDRRMF Utilization Report and submit the same to the auditor within the period required by regulation.
observations.managementAction: Monthly and quarterly reports on the utilization of MDRRMF were submitted to the Auditor’s Office regularly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.	Require the GAD Focal Point System/Focal Person to ensure that the Municipality’s GPB is prepared and appropriately evaluated by the PPDO to ensure that PPAs to be undertaken are in line with the GAD priority agenda of the Provincial Government and that activities are sufficiently reviewed and approved by the DILG; and
b.	Intensify GAD focal point persons on each department with trainings and seminars regarding GAD planning and budgeting concepts to enhance and broaden their knowledge in planning and proper execution of GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget was formulated by all heads of offices and planning officers as guided by the MLGOO. Said plan was submitted to the DILG (province and region) through the MLGOO for further review and approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management not allow any of its officials and employees’ monthly net pay go below P3,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Management reduced the number of employees with monthly net pay below P3,000.00. Further granting of loans to individuals that would result to receipt of less than the statutory net pay is no longer allowed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management procure its common-use goods or regular office supplies thru competitive bidding or other modes of procurement after determination of its non-availability from the Procurement Service and a certification from the PS-DBM has been acquired.
observations.managementAction: The LGU procured its common-used goods thru competitive bidding and other modes of procurement after determination of its non-availability in the DBM Procurement Service depot and certification from it has been acquired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned personnel substantiate their monthly cellcard allowance with Official Receipt in order not to be included as part of their compensation income.
observations.managementAction: Official Receipts were now attached in the disbursements of cellcard allowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer replace the stale checks if returned by creditors; or if not, revert the amount to payable accounts. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect reliable amounts in the cash and payable accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management advised the Treasury Office to act on the issue and necessary adjustments were already made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

Management observe strictly the general limitations on personnel services cited under Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160 and Section 1.1.1 of LBC No. 68;
The Municipal Mayor being the Local Chief Executive always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements were completely complied with; and
The Municipal Mayor have the political will to allow the payment of personnel costs within the personnel services limitations to avoid the disallowances and any sanction imposed in violating the above-cited laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Excess in the Personnel Service cost level significantly decreased.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 2, Page 34, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that officials/employees on official travel obtain a certification from the head of the agency to justify the necessity of the hotel/lodging expense if such is in excess of what is set by law, and non-government seminars/trainings with registration fees that is in excess of the prescribed rate shall be at the expense of the participant.
observations.managementAction: Incurrence of expenses in excess of what is prescribed by law were justified by attaching a certification about the necessity of the transaction from the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management discontinue extending financial assistance to religious and private institutions to achieve the objectives behind the prohibition.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the audit recommendation and discontinued extending financial assistances to religious and private institutions.

observations.managementAction: Cash advances for travels and trainings were supported with complete documentary requirements.

A MOA was already signed by both parties.
